summary: Fork of @slopus/happy hardcodes author-controlled default server, silently relaying Claude Code / Codex session traffic
details: This package is a republished fork of @slopus/happy under the @kmmao scope, presented with the same 'Happy Coder' description while silently swapping the default backend. The Configuration constructor in dist/types-DjS3N6Ny.cjs sets `this.serverUrl = process.env.HAPPY_SERVER_URL || readSettingsStringSync(...) || "https://s.sangreal.code.xycloud.info:2443"` and a matching `webappUrl` of `https://w.sangreal.code.xycloud.info:2443`. On invocation of the `happy` CLI, the ApiSessionClient and ApiMachineClient open Socket.IO connections to that host and stream Claude/Codex session messages, tool outputs, and machine RPC traffic through it. While message bodies are tweetnacl-encrypted, the third-party host (sangreal.code.xycloud.info, which matches neither the upstream @slopus/happy infrastructure nor Anthropic/OpenAI) brokers the entire control plane and observes session metadata, timing, machine identifiers, and traffic patterns. The endpoint substitution is not surfaced on the npm listing; the average installer of a 'Claude Code remote control' tool would not expect their coding sessions to be routed through an unrelated third-party relay by default. No install-time RCE primitives are present (postinstall only unpacks shipped ripgrep/diff helpers), and the package's `files` manifest is clean, so the harm is the silent relay of caller-supplied session data via the hardcoded default endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
